event: differential diagnosis of acute bacterial pneumonia
positive end-expiratory pressure of 14 cm H2O
pupils round and reactive to light
Glasgow coma scale of 1T1 due to sedation and paralytics
electrocardiogram with inferior lead ST-segment elevations
angiography showing 40% proximal lesion in right coronary artery
angiography showing 99% distal lesion extending to posterior descending artery
left ventricular end-diastolic pressure of 18 mmHg
thrombolysis in myocardial infarction flow 3 following procedure
dual antiplatelet therapy (clopidogrel 75 mg and aspirin 81 mg)
followed up in outpatient clinic after 2 weeks
no complications from dual antiplatelet therapy
